items.title: Unit coverage — draft pinning + verify net
items.expected: All tests pass: resolvePageDraftId pins one draft per path (read+write share a row, idempotent across calls); extractAddedTokens/changeIsPresent flag an absent change and confirm a present one.
items.evidence: 10/10 passed (full suite 83/83). check:conventions --strict: 0 new backend violations. tsc: 0 errors from changed files.
items.notes: Layer 1 automated coverage of the core helpers.

items.title: Multi-edit on one section both persist (the bug)
items.precondition: Deployed to dev. A website project with a section that has two editable targets (e.g. a hero with a CTA link AND a label).
steps: 1. Open Admin > Website > Advanced Tools > AI Command (AI Editor).
2. Run a bulk instruction that hits the same section twice, e.g. 'change all cal.com links to /book-a-demo, and rename Free Checkup to Free Audit'.
3. Approve all, Execute, wait for Completed.
4. Open the live/published page for that section.
items.expected: BOTH changes are present on the published page — the link shows /book-a-demo AND the label shows Free Audit. Neither overwrites the other.
items.notes: Pre-fix this produced only the last write per section (link reverted to cal.com).
items.waiver: Deferred Layer-2 UI verification — runs on dev.getalloro.com after this push deploys, by the owner (frontend-visual-pass workflow). Not a defect: the core draft-pinning logic is proven deterministically by Layer-1 unit tests (T3, 10/10), and the same outcome was demonstrated by the 2026-06-26 prod data repair. Waived for finalization per owner -d.

items.title: An edit that doesn't land is reported failed, not executed
items.precondition: Deployed to dev. Same AI Command surface.
steps: 1. Run a bulk edit whose change cannot reach the published section (e.g. a section whose targeted index drifts, or force a no-land case).
3. Inspect the batch result counts / recommendation statuses.
items.expected: The recommendation whose change did not reach the published content shows as failed (with a verify_reason), not executed. Batch stats reflect the failure rather than a false success.
items.waiver: Deferred Layer-2 UI verification — runs on dev after this push deploys, by the owner. Not a defect: the verify-net detection logic (changeIsPresent flags an absent change) is proven by Layer-1 unit tests (T3). Waived for finalization per owner -d.
